pairs.question: What was the CMLL 68th Anniversary Show and when did it take place?
pairs.answer: The CMLL 68th Anniversary Show was a professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by Consejo Mundial de Lucha Libre (CMLL) that commemorated the 68th anniversary of the promotion. It took place on September 28, 2001, in Arena México, Mexico City, Mexico.

pairs.question: Why is the CMLL Anniversary Show considered significant in professional wrestling?
pairs.answer: The CMLL Anniversary Show is significant because it is CMLL's biggest event of the year, often compared to a Super Bowl, and it holds the record for the longest-running annual professional wrestling show, starting in 1934.

pairs.question: How many matches were part of the CMLL 68th Anniversary Show, and what was the main event?
pairs.answer: The CMLL 68th Anniversary Show consisted of five matches in total. The main event was a Lucha de Apuestas, elimination steel cage match where seven competitors put their masks on the line. The winner also earned the right to use the name 'Los Infernales'.

pairs.question: Who were the competitors in the steel cage match at the CMLL 68th Anniversary Show?
pairs.answer: The competitors in the steel cage match were Último Guerrero, Rey Bucanero, Tarzan Boy, Máscara Mágica, Mephisto, Averno, and El Satánico.

pairs.question: What was unique about the steel cage match at the CMLL 68th Anniversary Show?
pairs.answer: This was the first time a CMLL Anniversary Show featured a steel cage match, making it a historic event in the promotion's history.

pairs.question: Which championship was defended at the CMLL 68th Anniversary Show, and who were the involved teams?
pairs.answer: The Mexican National Trios Championship was defended at the event. Olímpico, Mr. Niebla, and Safari defended the title against Los Boricuas, consisting of Gran Markus Jr., Violencia, and Poder Boricua.
